title: docs(handoffs): PR #177 (CI-RED-020-B) merge-instability handoff
intent: Add a durable dev handoff explaining why PR #177 read `mergeStateStatus: UNSTABLE` while MERGEABLE — a transient pending-window on non-required Core CI jobs, rooted in CI-RED-021 (no branch protection: nothing is required, so `UNSTABLE` is the default steady state, never `CLEAN` while a slow/advisory job is pending or red), NOT a defect in #177. Records the UNSTABLE-vs-BLOCKED distinction, the merge decision guidance, the 020-B closure bar, and the systemic 'read status per-check' takeaway. Documentation only; no product/application/CI-behavior change. This manifest replaces the inherited code manifest (not applicable to a docs-only diff) so the CBSP21 gates cover this PR.

diff_articulation.what_changed: Added docs/handoffs/PR_177_CI_RED_020B_MERGE_INSTABILITY_HANDOFF.md — a standalone dev handoff: (1) the GitHub merge-state model (CLEAN/UNSTABLE/BLOCKED/DIRTY) and why UNSTABLE-vs-BLOCKED turns on whether a check is *required*; (2) this repo has no branch protection (CI-RED-021) so nothing is required, making UNSTABLE the default steady state; (3) on #177 the instability was a transient pending-window on 3 non-required Core CI jobs (API Tests x2, api-verify) whose outcomes are independent of #177's CI-only diff and reflect main's (green, post-#176) state; (4) the fixed workflow triggers on schedule/dispatch only, so it is not a PR check — its proof is out-of-band (branch dispatch runs + the post-merge scheduled run); (5) merge decision guidance and the 020-B closure bar; (6) a same-day status note that the window resolved green -> CLEAN -> #177 merged (`b8ebc552`). Also replaced .cbsp21/patch_input.json with this docs-handoff manifest declaring the added file so the CBSP21 scope + coverage gates cover this PR.

commands_run: local: python scripts/ci/check_cbsp21_patch_input.py --base origin/main --head HEAD -> PASS (handoff file declared in scope).

files.intent: Durable handoff explaining PR #177's UNSTABLE merge state (transient pending Core CI; root cause CI-RED-021 no branch protection) and how to read/merge such PRs.
